title: corynocarpus rupestris
intro: Corynocarpus rupestris, also known as the Matagouri, is a shrub-like plant that is native to the coastal regions of New Zealand. It has been traditionally used in Maori medicine for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Corynocarpus rupestris include its ability to soothe skin irritations and wounds, as well as its potential to help lower blood pressure and reduce the risk of cardiovascular disease. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and topical creams, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using any plant-based remedy.
